$comment: Pyxis M10 image-regression fixture for the World Lobby hero camera. Consumed by _tools/run_regression.py. Plan §35.
name: M10.WorldLobbyRegression
description: Lobby hero camera, M9-fidelity stable baseline. Standin until World Lobby lands; same harness will retarget World Lobby by editing the config + scene fields below.
$tolerance-comment: Initial tolerances are deliberately loose. With §36.5's strict-mode contract (RTX 4080 + pinned driver + pinned Vulkan SDK + Win 11 23H2/24H2) byte-identical EXRs are achievable; outside that matrix per-test floats kick in. M10 ships tolerant-mode defaults so the test goes green on any developer workstation; CI's nightly tightens via _tools/regression_matrix.json when M10's perf-tracking phase lands.

Values express normalised float-EXR pixel-delta — i.e. 0.02 RMSE means 2% of the 0..1 dynamic range averaged across all RGB+A samples. The dome's HDRI lift makes some pixels HDR (> 1.0); maxAbsoluteDelta therefore needs headroom.
